title: Search Head Cluster Status
spl:
| rest /services/shcluster/member/members
| table label, status, last_heartbeat, replication_count
| eval heartbeat_age=now()-last_heartbeat
| where status!="Up" OR heartbeat_age > 300
description: SHC member failures affect user access and search capacity. Captain election issues can cause complete SHC outage.
value: SHC member failures affect user access and search capacity. Captain election issues can cause complete SHC outage.
grandmaExplanation: SHC member failures affect user access and search capacity — so you always trust the numbers you are looking at.
implementation: Monitor SHC member health via REST API. Track captain status and election events. Alert on member disconnection or replication failures. Monitor artifact replication lag between members.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` (SHC logs, REST endpoints).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor SHC member health via REST API. Track captain status and election events. Alert on member disconnection or replication failures. Monitor artifact replication lag between members.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/shcluster/member/members
| table label, status, last_heartbeat, replication_count
| eval heartbeat_age=now()-last_heartbeat
| where status!="Up" OR heartbeat_age > 300
```

Understanding this SPL

**Search Head Cluster Status** — SHC member failures affect user access and search capacity. Captain election issues can cause complete SHC outage.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` (SHC logs, REST endpoints). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Pipeline stage (see **Search Head Cluster Status**): table label, status, last_heartbeat, replication_count
• `eval` defines or adjusts **heartbeat_age** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where status!="Up" OR heartbeat_age > 300` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (SHC member × status), Table (member health), Timeline (captain election events).
visualization: Status grid (SHC member × status), Table (member health), Timeline (captain election events).
